issues.body: The new text calls the cooperative row/byte/cancel limits plus timeout "the worst-case bound for embedded deployments." That guarantee is not provided. QueryBudget is polled only at coarse executor sites, and the accompanying skill documentation explicitly says index-answerable and tiny early-exit shapes may complete before the first poll. In addition, max_bytes is an estimated lower bound that excludes allocator overhead and other memory, and window graph materialisation happens before the prepared-query budget is installed. Thus neither wall-clock time nor total memory has a strict worst-case bound.
issues.fix_hint: Replace the worst-case-bound statement with a precise cooperative-limit claim. State that timeout/cancellation take effect at the next executor poll, max_bytes limits an estimated query working set rather than total process/window-materialisation memory, and some bounded-work paths may finish before polling. Keep the concise README wording consistent with the caveats already present in skills/streaming-rsp/SKILL.md.
issues.title: README falsely promises a worst-case deployment bound

summary: The budget is correctly threaded through the prepared SELECT, CONSTRUCT, ASK, and multi-window evaluation seams, and the tests exercise real executor polling paths. However, the README materially overstates the guarantee: cooperative budgets with coarse polling do not provide a worst-case wall-clock or memory bound. The more detailed skill text already acknowledges that some bounded-work query shapes may finish without polling, while QueryBudget's byte accounting is documented as only a lower-bound estimate of heap use. This public embedded-deployment claim should be corrected before approval.
